metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in Hackney! I've found some fantastic activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from puppet shows at the Little Angel Theatre to exploring the Young V&A. There's even a Candlelight Concert for Babies for a calm sensory experience.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: A little old lady's cottage feels too small, so she brings in farmyard animals. Based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets, farm animals, and a familiar story make this engaging for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: Candlelight Concert for Babies
activities.venue: The Great Hall, City of London
activities.description: Gentle classical music in a softly lit, candle-style setting designed to be calm and sensory-friendly.
activities.whyItWorks: A calming sensory experience perfect for babies.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to St. Paul's, then a short walk.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Museum with collections of toys, objects and artworks. Galleries designed for children to imagine, play and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays, open studio, and space for performance. Moving and Making gallery for active play.

transport.fromHackney: Buses 106, 254, 388 and D6.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a quiet and engaging space for children of all ages to explore books and stories.

transport.fromHackney: Local buses and trains are available.

activities.title: Family Day: Cosy Critters
activities.description: Weave a cosy bed for a dragon, songbird or bunny on a cardboard loom.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on crafting activity suitable for a wide range of ages.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow Central, then a short walk.

activities.title: Beauty and Destruction: Wartime London in Art
activities.description: Exhibition featuring a 'stamper' trail and activity sheet that encourages children to engage with the artworks.
activities.whyItWorks: Activity sheet makes the exhibition engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Lambeth North or Waterloo, then short walk.
transport.tip: Check IWM website for opening times and any special events.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: A little old lady's cottage feels too small, so she brings in farmyard animals. Based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets, farm animals, and a familiar story make this engaging for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: Planet Play at Chickenshed
activities.description: Interactive, sensory show.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory experience designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Check public transport options for Chickenshed.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Motivates kids to walk with the promise of chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore the Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with an engaging Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town, then short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online for best prices.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission. An interactive trail to complete challenges and learn about protecting the planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor adventure with a familiar theme and educational elements.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Kew Gardens station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Easter Activities
activities.description: The Elephant Trail, LEGO® Build Event, River Walk Market and Salad Days Market. The Chimney Lift will host a Charlie the Chimney Easter Event from 28th March – 12th April.
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of activities for different interests and age ranges.

transport.fromHackney: Zone 1 Battersea Power Station Underground station.
transport.tip: Check the Battersea Power Station website for a detailed schedule.

activities.title: Ham House and Garden Easter Trail
activities.description: Enjoy a seasonal trail set within one of south-west London's most intact 17th-century estates.
activities.whyItWorks: Self-guided outdoor activity with interactive tasks.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Richmond, then bus or walk to Ham House.

activities.title: Fulham Palace Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Self-guided trail weaves through historic palace interiors and surrounding gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Exploration of a historic site with a fun Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Putney Bridge, then short walk.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Award-winning musical, a favorite with families in London.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stunning and features familiar characters.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books come to life on stage with Bear, Little Fish and the Turtles.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry brings familiar characters to life.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: Easter Holidays at V&A South Kensington
activities.description: Easter Holidays Festival.
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of activities suitable for different age groups.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for a detailed schedule.

activities.title: Family Design Challenge
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Road, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
activities.description: Family Design Challenge.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative, hands-on activity.

transport.fromHackney: Buses 106, 254, 388 and D6.
transport.tip: Check Young V&A website for details.

activities.title: Gabby's Dollhouse Live!
activities.description: Stage show brings Gabby and her dollhouse friends to life with songs and interactive moments.
activities.whyItWorks: Bright, colourful and designed for ages 2-6.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hammersmith, then short walk.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond
activities.description: Enjoy a family Easter adventure exploring installations to find bees and help them build a new hive.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and encourages outdoor exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Kew Gardens.

activities.title: The Enormous Crocodile
activities.description: Children's theatre show.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Check public transport options for Kingston.

activities.title: Family Workshops - Little Lambs
activities.description: Get woolly at the Little Lamb Family Workshop for Springtime.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and hands-on crafting activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: Puppet Playcation - Superheroes
activities.description: Spend the day making your very own superhero puppet.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and engaging for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.
